channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - techraptor.net
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for techraptor.net

item.title: Valve Reveals Steam Machine Prices at Over $1000
item.content:encoded:
Ever since it was revealed, we've been waiting for more details on Valve's Steam Machines, how much they would cost and what they would contain as far as hardware would concern. With a situation in the tech world of inflated prices and shortages driven by (evil) AI data centers, the situation had put Valve in a bind where they had previously hinted the cost was going to be more than they liked, which was confirmed today.

How Much Does the Steam Machine Cost?

The Steam Machine comes in 4 packages, but its cheapest version costs $1049 US and comes with 512 GB SSD memory. This version comes with no controller and provides just the box itself. Note that if you're wanting to separately order the Steam Controller, reservations for that are running into an unspecified time in 2027. Here are the other options for buying the Steam Machine:

  • Steam Machine 512GB SSD With Controller: $1128 USD
  • Steam Machine 2TB SSD No Controller: $1349 USD
  • Steam Machine 2TD SSD With Controller: $1428 USD

The two terabyte versions of the Steam Machine come with two additional faceplates of red fabric and solid walnut if the black box look of Valve's design doesn't impress you.

What are the Steam Machine's Specs?

Outside of SSD size and faceplates, the specs of both versions of the machine are the same so we can run them down here quickly from Valve:

  • Semi-custom AMD Zen 4 6C / 12T
  • Semi-custom AMD RDNA3 28CUs
  • 16GB DDR5 + 8GB GDDR6 VRAM
  • 512GB (Or 2 TB) NVMe SSD, microSD card slot
  • Wi-Fi 6E, Bluetooth 5.3, Gigabit ethernet
  • Integrated Steam Controller wireless adapter
  • Small form factor, ~6 inch cube 

How can you order a Steam Machine?

To order a steam machine you sign up to get the machine or model you want to buy. If you do so by Thursday June 25th, at 10 a.m. Pacific, you'll be put in a randomized lottery that will set a list for ordering queue and you'll receive an email about your location in it, and when you can buy the Steam Machine. The first set of Steam Machines will be shipped out on Monday, June 29th, and they'll continue going through the list as supply becomes available. After the 25th, everyone will be added to the back of the line and fulfilled subsequently.

That supply is apparently quite limited for launch, as the component shortage has caused them difficulty there. The price of the machine is, according to Valve, based on the cost of components they've secured over the past 6 months and there have been times they haven't been able to source some components at all. 

A longtime lover of speculative fiction, in almost all its forms, Don joined TechRaptor in 2014 on a whim sending in an application as he was looking for… More about Don
